meaning: adj.有害的,恶作剧的,淘气的,为害
examples: Berg tilts his head and a mischievous look crosses his face.
She rocks back and forth on her chair like a mischievous child.
When poverty shows itself, even mischievous boys understand what it means.
Speaking Saturday night at the GLAAD Media Awards, Madonna started off in a mischievous mood.
The British government regards it as a mischievous attempt to pull Britain away from Europe.
